story 5

chingiz rabdanov

2026-04-20

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)


# loading library

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
## Warning: пакет 'ggpubr' был собран под R версии 4.5.3
library(corrplot)
## Warning: пакет 'corrplot' был собран под R версии 4.5.3
## corrplot 0.95 loaded
library(scales)
## 
## Присоединяю пакет: 'scales'
## 
## Следующий объект скрыт от 'package:purrr':
## 
##     discard
## 
## Следующий объект скрыт от 'package:readr':
## 
##     col_factor
library(janitor)
## Warning: пакет 'janitor' был собран под R версии 4.5.3
## 
## Присоединяю пакет: 'janitor'
## 
## Следующие объекты скрыты от 'package:stats':
## 
##     chisq.test, fisher.test
# loading datasets from GitHub

url <- ("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chingiz1492/temperature/refs/heads/main/1950-2019_all_tornadoes.csv")

url1 <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chingiz1492/temperature/refs/heads/main/GLB.Ts%2BdSST.csv"

url2 <- ("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Chingiz1492/temperature/refs/heads/main/ibtracs_2010_2025.csv")

url3 <- "https://www.spc.noaa.gov/wcm/data/1950-2024_all_tornadoes.csv"
 
tornado_dataset    <- read_csv(url,  show_col_types = FALSE)
temperture_dataset <- read_csv(url1, show_col_types = FALSE)
## Warning: One or more parsing issues, call `problems()` on your data frame for details,
## e.g.:
##   dat <- vroom(...)
##   problems(dat)
storm_dataset      <- read_csv(url2, show_col_types = FALSE)
tornado_dataset2   <- read_csv(url3, show_col_types = FALSE)
 
# Merge tornado datasets and remove duplicates
tornado_dataset <- bind_rows(tornado_dataset, tornado_dataset2) %>%
  distinct()
 
cat("tornado_dataset total rows:", nrow(tornado_dataset), "\n")
## tornado_dataset total rows: 74247
# ── 3. Prepare: temperture_dataset → temp_clean ──────────────────────────────
temp_clean <- temperture_dataset %>%
  slice(-1) %>%
  separate(
    col  = `Land-Ocean: Global Means`,
    into = c("year","Jan","Feb","Mar","Apr","May","Jun",
             "Jul","Aug","Sep","Oct","Nov","Dec",
             "J-D","D-N","DJF","MAM","JJA","SON"),
    sep  = ","
  ) %>%
  rename(annual_temp = `J-D`) %>%
  mutate(
    year        = as.integer(year),
    annual_temp = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(annual_temp))
  ) %>%
  select(year, annual_temp) %>%
  filter(!is.na(annual_temp), year >= 2010, year <= 2025)
 
# ── 4. Prepare: tornado_dataset → tornado_annual ──────────────────────────────
tornado_annual <- tornado_dataset %>%
  filter(mag >= 0, yr >= 2010, yr <= 2025) %>%
  group_by(year = yr) %>%
  summarise(
    tornado_count      = n(),
    tornado_avg_mag    = mean(mag, na.rm = TRUE),
    tornado_injuries   = sum(inj,  na.rm = TRUE),
    tornado_fatalities = sum(fat,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
 
# ── 5. Prepare: storm_dataset → cyclone_annual ───────────────────────────────
cyclone_annual <- storm_dataset %>%
  mutate(
    year     = as.integer(SEASON),
    wmo_wind = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(WMO_WIND)),
    wmo_pres = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(WMO_PRES))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(wmo_wind), wmo_wind > 0, year >= 2010, year <= 2025) %>%
  group_by(year, SID) %>%
  summarise(
    peak_wind = max(wmo_wind, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_pres  = min(wmo_pres, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    cyclone_count    = n(),
    cyclone_avg_wind = mean(peak_wind, na.rm = TRUE),
    cyclone_max_wind = max(peak_wind,  na.rm = TRUE),
    cyclone_avg_pres = mean(min_pres,  na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
 
# ── 6. Join datasets ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_tornado <- temp_clean %>%
  inner_join(tornado_annual, by = "year")
 
df_cyclone <- temp_clean %>%
  inner_join(cyclone_annual, by = "year")
 
cat("df_tornado:", nrow(df_tornado), "years\n")
## df_tornado: 15 years
cat("df_cyclone:", nrow(df_cyclone), "years\n")
## df_cyclone: 16 years
# ── 7. Pearson Correlation Analysis ──────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nCORRELATIONS: annual_temp ~ tornado_dataset\n")
## 
## CORRELATIONS: annual_temp ~ tornado_dataset
for (v in c("tornado_count", "tornado_avg_mag",
            "tornado_injuries", "tornado_fatalities")) {
  ct <- cor.test(df_tornado$annual_temp, df_tornado[[v]], method = "pearson")
  cat(sprintf("  %-28s r = %+.3f  p = %.4f\n", v, ct$estimate, ct$p.value))
}
##   tornado_count                r = +0.166  p = 0.5534
##   tornado_avg_mag              r = +0.331  p = 0.2277
##   tornado_injuries             r = -0.403  p = 0.1369
##   tornado_fatalities           r = -0.392  p = 0.1486
cat("\nCORRELATIONS: annual_temp ~ storm_dataset\n")
## 
## CORRELATIONS: annual_temp ~ storm_dataset
for (v in c("cyclone_count", "cyclone_avg_wind",
            "cyclone_max_wind", "cyclone_avg_pres")) {
  ct <- cor.test(df_cyclone$annual_temp, df_cyclone[[v]], method = "pearson")
  cat(sprintf("  %-28s r = %+.3f  p = %.4f\n", v, ct$estimate, ct$p.value))
}
##   cyclone_count                r = -0.088  p = 0.7455
##   cyclone_avg_wind             r = +0.339  p = 0.1991
##   cyclone_max_wind             r = +0.549  p = 0.0276
##   cyclone_avg_pres             r = -0.078  p = 0.7737
# ── 8. Plot theme ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
theme_custom <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "grey45"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
 
# ── 9. Plot 1: Global temperature time series ─────────────────────────────────
ggplot(temp_clean, aes(year, annual_temp)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.5, color = "firebrick", se = TRUE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  labs(
    title    = "Global Surface Temperature Anomaly (temperture_dataset)",
    subtitle = "NASA GISS Land-Ocean Temperature Index · Baseline 1951–1980",
    x = "Year", y = "annual_temp (°C)"
  ) +
  theme_custom
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 10. Plot 2: tornado_count ~ annual_temp ───────────────────────────────────
ggplot(df_tornado, aes(annual_temp, tornado_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "darkorange") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "tornado_count ~ annual_temp",
    subtitle = "tornado_dataset · USA 2010–2024",
    x = "annual_temp (°C)", y = "tornado_count"
  ) +
  theme_custom
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 11. Plot 3: tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp ─────────────────────────────────
ggplot(df_tornado, aes(annual_temp, tornado_avg_mag)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "purple") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp",
    subtitle = "tornado_dataset · Fujita / Enhanced Fujita scale",
    x = "annual_temp (°C)", y = "tornado_avg_mag"
  ) +
  theme_custom
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 12. Plot 4: cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp ────────────────────────────────
ggplot(df_cyclone, aes(annual_temp, cyclone_avg_wind)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = cyclone_count), alpha = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top") +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "cyclone_count") +
  labs(
    title    = "cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp",
    subtitle = "storm_dataset · IBTrACS 2010–2025 (all basins)",
    x = "annual_temp (°C)", y = "cyclone_avg_wind (knots)"
  ) +
  theme_custom
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 13. Plot 5: cyclone_count ~ annual_temp ───────────────────────────────────
ggplot(df_cyclone, aes(annual_temp, cyclone_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "firebrick", se = TRUE) +
  stat_cor(method = "pearson", label.x.npc = "left", label.y.npc = "top") +
  labs(
    title    = "cyclone_count ~ annual_temp",
    subtitle = "storm_dataset · IBTrACS 2010–2025",
    x = "annual_temp (°C)", y = "cyclone_count"
  ) +
  theme_custom
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 14. Plot 6: Time-series dashboard (tornado_dataset) ───────────────────────
df_tornado %>%
  select(year, annual_temp, tornado_count, tornado_avg_mag) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(year, value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.5, se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    annual_temp     = "firebrick",
    tornado_count   = "darkorange",
    tornado_avg_mag = "purple"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Temperature & Tornado Trends · tornado_dataset (2010–2024)",
    x = "Year", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_custom +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# ── 15. Plot 7: Correlation matrix — tornado_dataset ──────────────────────────
df_tornado %>%
  select(annual_temp, tornado_count, tornado_avg_mag,
         tornado_injuries, tornado_fatalities) %>%
  cor(use = "complete.obs") %>%
  corrplot(
    method = "color", type = "upper",
    addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.85,
    tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 30,
    title = "Correlation Matrix — tornado_dataset",
    mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
  )

# ── 16. Plot 8: Correlation matrix — storm_dataset ────────────────────────────
df_cyclone %>%
  select(annual_temp, cyclone_count, cyclone_avg_wind,
         cyclone_max_wind, cyclone_avg_pres) %>%
  cor(use = "complete.obs") %>%
  corrplot(
    method = "color", type = "upper",
    addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.85,
    tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 30,
    title = "Correlation Matrix — storm_dataset",
    mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0)
  )

# ── 17. Linear Regression Models ──────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\nlm(tornado_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)\n")
## 
## lm(tornado_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)
print(summary(lm(tornado_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tornado_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -329.4 -244.7 -109.4  136.0  944.4 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)    961.1      481.4   1.997   0.0672 .
## annual_temp    325.0      534.3   0.608   0.5534  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 380.7 on 13 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02768,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.04711 
## F-statistic: 0.3701 on 1 and 13 DF,  p-value: 0.5534
cat("\nlm(tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)\n")
## 
## lm(tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)
print(summary(lm(tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = tornado_avg_mag ~ annual_temp, data = df_tornado)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.142939 -0.031924 -0.000838  0.035664  0.182786 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   0.5214     0.1127   4.627 0.000474 ***
## annual_temp   0.1584     0.1251   1.266 0.227665    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.08913 on 13 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1098, Adjusted R-squared:  0.04131 
## F-statistic: 1.603 on 1 and 13 DF,  p-value: 0.2277
cat("\nlm(cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)\n")
## 
## lm(cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)
print(summary(lm(cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = cyclone_avg_wind ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -7.8840 -3.3571  0.5683  3.3821  6.0235 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   59.728      5.158  11.579 1.48e-08 ***
## annual_temp    7.544      5.597   1.348    0.199    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 4.323 on 14 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1149, Adjusted R-squared:  0.05164 
## F-statistic: 1.817 on 1 and 14 DF,  p-value: 0.1991
cat("\nlm(cyclone_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)\n")
## 
## lm(cyclone_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)
print(summary(lm(cyclone_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = cyclone_count ~ annual_temp, data = df_cyclone)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -29.823  -7.586   3.021   9.565  15.210 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   99.219     14.965   6.630 1.13e-05 ***
## annual_temp   -5.375     16.236  -0.331    0.746    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 12.54 on 14 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.007766,   Adjusted R-squared:  -0.06311 
## F-statistic: 0.1096 on 1 and 14 DF,  p-value: 0.7455
# 18 visualisation 

 theme_pres <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#0A1628", size = 16),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#7FA7BE", size = 11, face = "italic"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    axis.text  = element_text(color = "#64748B"),
    axis.title = element_text(color = "#334E68"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

#  Temperature Trend ───────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(temp_clean, aes(x = year, y = annual_temp)) +
  geom_line(color = "#065A82", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(color = "#065A82", size = 2.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.5, color = "#F4A261",
              se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = annual_temp), vjust = -0.8, size = 3,
            color = "#065A82") +
  labs(
    title    = "Global Temperature Trend (2010–2024)",
    subtitle = "temp_clean · annual_temp from +0.61°C (2011) to record +1.28°C (2024)",
    x = "Year", y = "annual_temp (°C)",
    caption  = "Source: temperture_dataset · NASA GISS Land-Ocean Temperature Index"
  ) +
  theme_pres
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

# Tornado Count ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(df_tornado, aes(x = year, y = tornado_count)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#F4A261", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = tornado_count), vjust = -0.4, size = 3,
            color = "#334E68") +
  annotate("text", x = 2019, y = 2400,
           label = paste0("Pearson r = +", round(cor(df_tornado$annual_temp, df_tornado$tornado_count), 3)),
           color = "#065A82", fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title    = "tornado_count vs annual_temp",
    subtitle = "df_tornado · NOAA SPC · USA 2010–2024 · Avg = 1,249 tornadoes/year",
    x = "Year", y = "tornado_count",
    caption  = "Source: tornado_dataset · NOAA SPC"
  ) +
  theme_pres

# Tornado Magnitude ───────────────────────────────────────────────
df_tornado %>%
  select(year, tornado_avg_mag, annual_temp) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(c(
               annual_temp     = "annual_temp (°C)",
               tornado_avg_mag = "tornado_avg_mag (EF scale)"
             ))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    annual_temp     = "#065A82",
    tornado_avg_mag = "#9333EA"
  )) +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf,
           label = "Pearson r = +0.330", hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5,
           color = "#9333EA", fontface = "bold", size = 4) +
  labs(
    title    = "tornado_avg_mag vs annual_temp",
    subtitle = "df_tornado · Fujita / EF scale · Pearson r = +0.330",
    x = "Year", y = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: tornado_dataset"
  ) +
  theme_pres +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#0A1628"))

# Cyclone Wind Speed ──────────────────────────────────────────────
df_cyclone %>%
  select(year, cyclone_max_wind, cyclone_avg_wind) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, color = variable)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(cyclone_max_wind = "#E63946", cyclone_avg_wind = "#1C7293"),
    labels = c(cyclone_max_wind = "cyclone_max_wind (knots)",
               cyclone_avg_wind = "cyclone_avg_wind (knots)")
  ) +
  annotate("text", x = 2011, y = 188,
           label = "r (max wind vs temp) = +0.549",
           color = "#E63946", fontface = "bold", size = 3.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 2011, y = 180,
           label = "r (avg wind vs temp) = +0.342",
           color = "#1C7293", fontface = "bold", size = 3.8) +
  labs(
    title    = "cyclone_avg_wind & cyclone_max_wind vs annual_temp",
    subtitle = "df_cyclone · storm_dataset · IBTrACS 2010–2025 · All ocean basins",
    x = "Year", y = "Wind speed (knots)",
    color    = NULL,
    caption  = "Source: storm_dataset · IBTrACS"
  ) +
  theme_pres +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
